REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (452 Dellbrook Avenue, San Francisco)
Off Calendar Aug-24-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mary G. Sancimino, via CourtCall, for and with June P. Allegro, via Zoom, Administrator, Jimmy Dou, in court, in propria persona, Agent for Buyer.

The court advises Ms. Sancimino that it appears that notice to the buyers regarding this hearing is defective as it was not provided at least 15 days prior to this hearing and that the buyers are not present today to waive notice. Ms. Sancimino offers testimony regarding service of the Notice of Hearing. Mr. Dou reports that his clients are aware of the hearing today and were not aware that they should be present. After consideration of the preceding, the court advises that it is waiving the notice defect.

The sale was confirmed at $1,042,800 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). Bond is not required.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Final Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Aug-09-2022 Continued To Aug-30-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (452 Dellbrook Avenue, San Francisco)
Off Calendar Jul-26-2022 Continued To Aug-23-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mary Sancimino, via Courtcall, for June Allegro, Administrator.

Counsel is advised regarding the defects in the petition and of the lack of proof of publication. Counsel advises that she intends to file an amended petition and proof of publication and advises that she expects to have an Inventory and Appraisal (I&A) filed soon. The court advises that the I&A needs to be filed prior to amending the petition.

The matter is continued to Aug-23-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-13-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: June Allegro, via zoom, in propria persona, Petitioner.

The court discusses with Petitioner her duties and liabilities as Administrator, as described in the Duties and Liabilities she filed with the court, should her petition be granted. The court and Petitioner discuss why she believes she will be able to administer the estate without an attorney. Petitioner offers testimony regarding her work history and reports that her husband is an attorney and has experience with probate matters. The court points to the various rules that she will be expected to adhere to especially opening an insured estate account to segregate estate assets, pay valid creditors, and not make any distributions without a court order. The court confirms that if appointed, Petitioner plans to sell the real property. The court advises Petitioner that the court is inclined to order a bond of $135,000, bond waivers notwithstanding. The court advises Petitioner that it can grant the order with a bond or take the matter under submission to consider the bond issue. Petitioner confirms she'd rather have the court take the matter under submission and reconsider the bond issue.

The Petition for Letters of Administration is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Cristobal. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-08-2022 Continued To Mar-29-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
